meta.title: Fed Signal Tracker
meta.description: Curated FOMC meeting log cross-referencing statement language shifts with rate outcomes and Treasury-market reactions.
meta.author: Christopher Givan
meta.methodology: Same-day (2pm statement to 3pm close) yield changes sourced from Treasury.gov H.15 series and cross-checked against Fed statement archive. Stance reflects the statement + SEP composite, not just the decision. This dataset is curated by hand — not an automated feed.
meta.coverage: Selected inflection meetings from the 2022 hiking cycle through the 2024 cut cycle.

meetings.statement_summary: Liftoff after two years at the zero lower bound. Signals an extended tightening path with multiple hikes through year-end.

phrase_changes.phrase: Ongoing increases in the target range will be appropriate
Expects to maintain this target range

meetings.notes: Statement delivered as expected but Powell's hawkish press conference pulled the entire curve higher. Dissent from Bullard for +50.

meetings.statement_summary: First 75 bp hike since 1994. Statement pivots from forward-guided hikes to data-dependence as May CPI printed 8.6%.

phrase_changes.phrase: Strongly committed to returning inflation to its 2 percent objective
Ongoing increases in the target range

meetings.notes: Counterintuitive rally — Powell's line that 75 bp 'would not be common' was read as a ceiling. Market was already priced for 75 via the WSJ leak.

meetings.statement_summary: Delivers fourth consecutive 75 bp hike but introduces 'cumulative tightening' and 'lags' language, flagging a slowdown in pace.

phrase_changes.phrase: Cumulative tightening of monetary policy, the lags with which monetary policy affects economic activity
Determining the pace of future increases

meetings.notes: Statement read dovish on first pass; Powell's presser erased it by flagging a higher terminal rate. Classic pace-vs-destination disconnect.

meetings.statement_summary: Downshifts to 25 bp. Statement replaces 'pace' of future increases with 'extent' — hinting the end of the cycle is in view.

phrase_changes.phrase: Pace of future increases → extent of future increases
Ongoing increases in the target range will be appropriate

meetings.notes: Powell's acknowledgment that the 'disinflationary process has started' drove a risk-on rally. Widely cited as the dovish pivot that aged poorly.

meetings.statement_summary: First hold of the cycle paired with a hawkish SEP — median dot moves to 5.6% implying two more hikes. Labeled 'skip' in the press conference.

phrase_changes.phrase: Holding the target range steady at this meeting allows the Committee to assess additional information
Additional policy firming may be appropriate

meetings.notes: Market largely priced the hold; the hawkish dots barely moved terminal rate pricing. The 'skip' framing let the Fed preserve optionality.

meetings.statement_summary: Pivot meeting. Inserts 'any' before 'additional policy firming' and the dot plot pencils in three 25 bp cuts for 2024.

phrase_changes.phrase: Additional policy firming → any additional policy firming
In determining the extent of any additional policy firming

meetings.notes: The 'any' is the highest-signal single word of the cycle. Two-year dropped nearly a basis point per minute for an hour. Powell did not push back on easing financial conditions.

meetings.statement_summary: Holds. SEP retains three cuts for 2024 despite hotter-than-expected Jan/Feb CPI. Powell stresses the bar to re-hike is high.

phrase_changes.phrase: Inflation has eased over the past year but remains elevated
Risks to achieving its employment and inflation goals are moving into better balance

meetings.notes: Market feared the dots would drop to two cuts; the 3-cut hold was received as a dovish surprise. Curve bull-steepened.

meetings.statement_summary: First cut of the easing cycle and it's a 50 bp 'insurance' move. Statement reframes mandate language as 'gained greater confidence' on inflation.

phrase_changes.phrase: Gained greater confidence that inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent
Judges that the risks to achieving its employment and inflation goals are roughly in balance

meetings.notes: Classic buy-the-rumor, sell-the-news. The front end backed up despite the larger cut because the SEP dots showed only 50 bp more in 2024. Dissent from Bowman for 25 bp.

meetings.statement_summary: Third straight cut paired with a hawkish SEP — 2025 median moved from four cuts to two. Statement adds 'extent and timing' hedge.

phrase_changes.phrase: Additional adjustments → extent and timing of additional adjustments

meetings.notes: Hawkish cut. Hammack dissented for a hold. The 'extent and timing' insertion mirrored the 'any' move in Dec 2023 but in reverse — same linguistic tell, opposite direction.

predictive_phrases.interpretation: Inserting 'any' before a tightening phrase is the Fed's most reliable public tell for an imminent pivot. Historically precedes the end of a hike cycle by 0–1 meetings.

predictive_phrases.phrase: extent and timing of additional adjustments
predictive_phrases.interpretation: Hedging both magnitude and schedule is a stronger slowdown signal than hedging magnitude alone. Coincided with a multi-meeting hold starting Jan 2025.
Confidence language has a short half-life — it shows up in the statement that contains the policy move, not the ones leading up to it.
Flags a pace change, not a direction change. Easy to misread as dovish when it is really a pivot to smaller, longer-duration hikes.
Appears when the Fed wants to reassert its anti-inflation mandate. Usually coincides with an upside policy surprise.
Shifts the conversation from 'how fast' to 'how much more.' Historically signals 1–2 hikes remaining.
Signals that labor-market weakness is now weighted alongside inflation. Precedes easing bias.

predictive_phrases.phrase: determining the pace of future increases
predictive_phrases.interpretation: Often gets headline treatment but does little to move the terminal rate. Better read as cover for the next SEP than as policy guidance.

research_notes.title: The 'any' tell
research_notes.body: The December 2023 insertion of 'any' before 'additional policy firming' is the cleanest single-word signal in the modern statement era. It traveled roughly -30 bp in the 2Y within minutes. The symmetric move in Dec 2024 ('extent and timing') produced the opposite trade with similar magnitude. Single-word edits in the forward-guidance paragraph tend to outperform SEP changes on same-day reaction.

research_notes.title: Statement vs. presser
research_notes.body: Statements are drafted by committee; press conferences are Powell. The Nov 2022 meeting is the textbook case — statement added dovish language ('cumulative tightening, lags') but Powell's presser flagged a higher terminal, flattening the curve 12 bp. The signal lives in both documents and the gap between them matters.

research_notes.title: Buy the rumor, sell the dots
research_notes.body: When markets have fully priced a decision, the reaction runs entirely through the SEP dot plot. Sept 2024 delivered a larger-than-expected cut but the dots showed only 50 more bp in 2024 — the front end sold off despite the dovish action. The dots are the marginal information when the decision itself is priced.

research_notes.title: Data caveat
research_notes.body: This dataset is hand-curated from the Federal Reserve statement archive and Treasury H.15 daily yields. It is not an automated feed. Each entry is verifiable against the FOMC statement and next-day H.15 release. Expansion targets: 2025 meetings, intraday reaction windows (30-min, 60-min), and Fed funds futures repricing.
